items.title: When Comedy Wasn't Enough - Jim Carrey
items.author: FilmStack
items.content:
<article>Thanks to Displate for sponsoring this video! 
Use our code FILMSTACK to get your Displates discounted or click on our link https://displate.com/@filmstack to get the discount automatically applied at checkout.

With Ace Ventura, The Mask and Dumb and Dumber all releasing within a single year, Jim Carrey was at the top of Hollywood. But for him it wasn't enough. Instead of playing it safer and only doing films like The Cable Guy and Bruce Almighty, Carrey wanted to prove he can do more than make people laugh by giving a go at more dramatic roles. No one would have ever thought that the slapstick king could star in films like The Truman Show, Man on the Moon, and Eternal Sunshine of the Spotless Mind, but that's what he did. 

But the transition into dramatic roles wasn't smooth, and he didn't really fit in a certain place in Hollywood. And as he got lost in his roles, he began to lose himself. So today, we’re exploring this transformative chapter of Jim Carrey’s career. What early struggles drove him to take such bold risks? How did he balance comedy and drama? And did his quest for more fulfill him? This is the story of when comedy wasn’t enough. This is Jim Carrey.

Chapters:
00:00 - Intro
02:34 - King of Comedy
07:27 - Leap Into Drama
12:19 - Uncertainty
16:00 - Legacy

Jim Carrey. Ace Ventura. The Mask. Cameron Diaz. Dumb and Dumber. Riddler. Batman Forever. The Cable Guy. Liar Liar. The Truman Show. Man on the Moon. The Majestic. How The Grinch Stole Christmas. Bruce Almighty. Me Myself and Irene. Bruce Almighty. Eternal Sunshine of the Spotless Mind. The Number 23. Lemony Snicket. Yes Man. Sonic the Hedgehog. Dumb and Dumber To. Mr Popper's Penguins. The Incredible Burt Wonderstone. No Oscar Nominations. Golden Globe Jim Carrey. 

If you like this video don't forget to leave a like, and if you're interested in videos about movies and the film industry in general, make sure to subscribe to FilmStack for more great content.

If you have any other ideas for videos, leave a comment and I might make a video with your idea.

Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/realfilmstack/

#jimcarrey     #comedyactor     #bestactor</article>

items.title: Impressions
items.author: RedLetterMedia
items.content: <article>Mike hosts a new game show where Josh, Jay and Rich Evans all are challenged to attempt impressions they have no idea how to do. Mike has randomly selected the impressions for them to do for his personal amusement.  Josh wonders how he got here and attempts to help Rich and Jay as they stumble through the challenges like perhaps children attempting to solve an advanced quantum mathematics problem presented by an MIT professor. It is a hopeless cause, but one worthy of laughing at their attempts right to their stupid faces. Will Josh win? Will Rich win? Will Jay win? Who knows! Also, there's a special bonus at the end of the video.</article>

items.title: THE SECOND | Animatic vs. Final (Bonus Video)
items.author: Every Frame a Painting
items.content:
<article>A side-by-side comparison of the animatic for “The Second” and the final film, with commentary provided by Taylor Ramos & Tony Zhou. Please enjoy, and we’ll see you for the next project.

Storyboarded by Taylor Ramos
Edited by Tony Zhou

Chapters
00:00 Introduction
01:08 Commentary Start, Realities of Production
02:09 Project Origins, Barry Lyndon, Creating Animatic
03:07 Working Process with Animatic
03:50 Green Screen PMP, 2nd Unit Reshoots
04:52 Zorro masks, long lenses, compromising with locations
06:07 Compressing multiple set-ups into one
06:59 Splitting up locations to break up movie visually
07:30 Duel negotiations
08:21 The Back to Back shot
09:01 Taylor’s plexiglass drawovers on set
09:33 Paul and Ethan in the argument scene
10:38 Composer, music cues
11:09 Tony’s composition vs. Taylor’s composition
11:31 Yes it was a real cloud passing his face
12:00 Painterly sequence, gun montage, VFX sky replacements
13:00 Subjectivity, no dialogue at beginning or end
13:50 Directing Ethan & Colton for the duel
14:40 Alfonso's moments of "ma," Taylor's first drawing
15:31 Deleted Shots & Scenes & Thank Yous

items.title: THE SECOND | Short Film
items.author: Every Frame a Painting
items.content:
<article>In an alternate version of today’s world where dueling is still acceptable, Philip, a man of tradition, must perform the role of “Second” on the day of his only son’s duel.

“The Second,” starring Paul Sun-Hyung Lee (Kim’s Convenience, The Mandalorian, Avatar: The Last Airbender) and Ethan Hwang (Riceboy Sleeps, Umbrella Academy) is the first live-action narrative short written & directed by Taylor Ramos and Tony Zhou. It premiered at Fantasia in 2024.

Credits:
Starring: Paul Sun-Hyung Lee, Ethan Hwang, Aaron Schwartz, Richard Wes, Colton Royce, Martin Ortega, Sam Osei, Tyrus Hwang

Written & Directed by: Taylor Ramos & Tony Zhou
Produced by: Nach Dudsdeemaytha, Jon Warne, Eric Bizzarri
Associate Producer: Rebeca Ortiz
Director of Photography: Alfonso Chin
Production Designer: Justin Campbell
Costume Designer: Asoomii Jay
Music by: Sean William
Sound Designer: May Guimarães
Storyboarded by: Taylor Ramos
Edited by: Tony Zhou
Casting by: Maureen Webb & Dylan Rogers</article>

items.content:
<article>If there’s one question that every filmmaker asks themselves, it’s “Where do I put the camera?” Today, we consider the things that really matter when answering that question.

A Video Essay by Taylor Ramos & Tony Zhou
Music by Sean William

items.title: The Story of Nosferatu: A Symphony of Horror (1922)
items.author: Layton Eversaul
items.content:
<article>In this in-depth retrospective, I tell the story behind one of cinema's towering classics: 1922's "Nosferatu: A Symphony of Horror". I dive deep into its development and historical context, its production, release and reception, legal troubles and complex legacy. Directed by F.W. Murnau, with Costumes & Sets by Albin Grau. Starring Max Schreck, Gustav Von Wongenheim, Greta Schröder,  and Alexander Granach. 

NOW ON PATREON: https://www.patreon.com/LaytonEversaul
Twitter: https://twitter.com/LaytonEversaul
Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/LaytonReviews
Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/laytoneversaul</article>

items.title: When an Actor Risked Everything - Matthew McConaughey
items.author: FilmStack
items.content:
<article>The McConaissance: the brief period in Matthew McConaughey’s career where he completely reinvented himself, doing a full 180 from the romcoms that defined his previous decade. With groundbreaking performances in Dallas Buyers Club, Interstellar, and True Detective it taught us a valuable lesson: never underestimate what an actor is truly capable of.

But McConaughey’s sudden career shift didn’t just impress us; it gave everyone whiplash. How did an actor at the top of the romcom world risk everything by stepping away from such lucrative roles? And why, after achieving one of Hollywood’s greatest comebacks, did this golden period fizzle out so quickly? That’s what we’re exploring today. What led McConaughey to even attempt this transformation? How did he execute it so flawlessly? And what happened after the McConaissance ended? This is the story of an actor who just decided to reinvent himself. This is Matthew McConaughey.


Chapters:
00:00 - Intro
00:56 - Rise & Typecasting
05:32 - Why and how?
08:05 - The McConaissance
12:45 - Peak McConaissance
18:05 - How did it end?

Matthew McConaughey. The McConaissance. Dazed and Confused. The Wolf of Wall Street. Leonardo DiCaprio. Contact. The Wedding Planner. How to lose a guy in 10 days. Sahara. Tropic Thunder. The Lincoln Lawyer. Bernie. Killer Joe. Dallas Buyers Club. Magic Mike. Mud. Interstellar. Rust Cohle. True Detective. The Dark Tower. Sing. Kubo and the Two Strings. Fool's Gold. Best Modern Actor. Martin Scorcese. Christopher Nolan. 

If you like this video don't forget to leave a like, and if you're interested in videos about movies and the film industry in general, make sure to subscribe to FilmStack for more great content.

If you have any other ideas for videos, leave a comment and I might make a video with your idea.

items.content:

They also saw Nosferatu.</article>

<article>#MarkKermode #SimonMayo #filmreviews 

A series of bank robberies and car heists frightened communities in the Pacific Northwest. A lone FBI agent believes that the crimes were not the work of financially motivated criminals, but rather a group of dangerous domestic terrorists.

To hear more, follow Kermode and Mayo's Take wherever you get your podcasts: https://listen.sonymusic-podcasts.link/TFTF28194?sid=kermodeandmayo.com</article>

items.title: Mark Kermode review How to Make Millions Before Grandma Dies - Kermode and Mayo's Take
items.author: Kermode and Mayo's Take
items.content:
<article>#MarkKermode #SimonMayo #filmreviews 

A man quits work to care for his dying grandmother, motivated by her fortune. He schemes to win her favor before she passes.

To hear more, follow Kermode and Mayo's Take wherever you get your podcasts: https://listen.sonymusic-podcasts.link/TFTF28194?sid=kermodeandmayo.com</article>
